provenance: Tu §7 Quotients, pdf 90-103 / printed 71-84, offset 19; hand-seed Track B r1

nodes.title: Quotients
nodes.text_raw: Gluing the edges of a malleable square is one way to create new surfaces. ... This gluing process is called an identification or a quotient construction. The main results of this section give conditions under which a quotient space remains second countable and Hausdorff.
nodes.text_normalized: Gluing the edges of a malleable square is one way to create new surfaces. ... This gluing process is called an identification or a quotient construction. The main results of this section give conditions under which a quotient space remains second countable and Hausdorff.

nodes.title: The Quotient Topology
nodes.text_raw: Recall that an equivalence relation on a set S is reflexive, symmetric, transitive ... the quotient topology on S/~ ...
nodes.text_normalized: Recall that an equivalence relation on a set S is reflexive, symmetric, transitive ... the quotient topology on S/~ ...

nodes.title: Continuity of a Map on a Quotient
heading_path: 7.2 Continuity of a Map on a Quotient
nodes.text_raw: Let ~ be an equivalence relation on the topological space S ... it induces a map f-bar : S/~ -> Y ...
nodes.text_normalized: Let ~ be an equivalence relation on the topological space S ... it induces a map f-bar : S/~ -> Y ...

nodes.title: Identification of a Subset to a Point
heading_path: 7.3 Identification of a Subset to a Point
nodes.text_raw: If A is a subspace of a topological space S ... identifying A to a point.
nodes.text_normalized: If A is a subspace of a topological space S ... identifying A to a point.

nodes.title: A Necessary Condition for a Hausdorff Quotient
heading_path: 7.4 A Necessary Condition for a Hausdorff Quotient
nodes.text_raw: The quotient construction does not in general preserve the Hausdorff property or second countability.
nodes.text_normalized: The quotient construction does not in general preserve the Hausdorff property or second countability.

nodes.title: Open Equivalence Relations
nodes.text_raw: In this section we follow the treatment of Boothby and derive conditions under which a quotient space is Hausdorff or second countable.
nodes.text_normalized: In this section we follow the treatment of Boothby and derive conditions under which a quotient space is Hausdorff or second countable.

nodes.title: Real Projective Space
nodes.text_raw: Define an equivalence relation on R^{n+1}-{0} ... The real projective space RP^n is the quotient space ...
nodes.text_normalized: Define an equivalence relation on R^{n+1}-{0} ... The real projective space RP^n is the quotient space ...

nodes.title: The Standard C-infinity Atlas on a Real Projective Space
heading_path: 7.7 The Standard C-infinity Atlas on a Real Projective Space
nodes.text_raw: The standard atlas on RP^n ...
nodes.text_normalized: The standard atlas on RP^n ...

nodes.label: Proposition 7.1
heading_path: 7.2 Continuity of a Map on a Quotient
nodes.text_raw: The induced map f-bar : S/~ -> Y is continuous if and only if the map f : S -> Y is continuous.
nodes.text_normalized: The induced map f-bar : S/~ -> Y is continuous if and only if the map f : S -> Y is continuous.

nodes.label: Example 7.2
heading_path: 7.3 Identification of a Subset to a Point
nodes.text_raw: Let I be the unit interval [0,1] and I/~ the quotient obtained from I by identifying {0,1} to a point ... induces f-bar : I/~ -> S^1.
nodes.text_normalized: Let I be the unit interval [0,1] and I/~ the quotient obtained from I by identifying {0,1} to a point ... induces f-bar : I/~ -> S^1.

nodes.label: Proposition 7.3
heading_path: 7.3 Identification of a Subset to a Point
nodes.text_raw: The function f-bar : I/~ -> S^1 is a homeomorphism.
nodes.text_normalized: The function f-bar : I/~ -> S^1 is a homeomorphism.

nodes.label: Proposition 7.4
heading_path: 7.4 A Necessary Condition for a Hausdorff Quotient
nodes.text_raw: If the quotient space S/~ is Hausdorff, then the equivalence class [p] of any point p in S is closed in S.
nodes.text_normalized: If the quotient space S/~ is Hausdorff, then the equivalence class [p] of any point p in S is closed in S.

nodes.label: Example
heading_path: 7.4 A Necessary Condition for a Hausdorff Quotient
nodes.text_raw: Define an equivalence relation ~ on R by identifying the open interval ]0,inf[ to a point. Then R/~ is not Hausdorff ...
nodes.text_normalized: Define an equivalence relation ~ on R by identifying the open interval ]0,inf[ to a point. Then R/~ is not Hausdorff ...

nodes.label: Definition 7.5
nodes.text_raw: An equivalence relation ~ on a topological space S is said to be open if the projection map pi : S -> S/~ is open.
nodes.text_normalized: An equivalence relation ~ on a topological space S is said to be open if the projection map pi : S -> S/~ is open.

nodes.label: Example 7.6
nodes.text_raw: The projection map to a quotient space is in general not open. For example, identify 1 and -1 on R ...
nodes.text_normalized: The projection map to a quotient space is in general not open. For example, identify 1 and -1 on R ...

nodes.label: Theorem 7.7
nodes.text_raw: Suppose ~ is an open equivalence relation on a topological space S. Then S/~ is Hausdorff if and only if the graph R of ~ is closed in S x S.
nodes.text_normalized: Suppose ~ is an open equivalence relation on a topological space S. Then S/~ is Hausdorff if and only if the graph R of ~ is closed in S x S.

nodes.label: Corollary 7.8
nodes.text_raw: A topological space S is Hausdorff if and only if the diagonal Delta in S x S is closed. (Found in round-1 ref-eval: cited 3x in §7 prose but missing from the first hand-seed; added.)
nodes.text_normalized: A topological space S is Hausdorff if and only if the diagonal Delta in S x S is closed. (Found in round-1 ref-eval: cited 3x in §7 prose but missing from the first hand-seed; added.)

nodes.label: Theorem 7.9
nodes.text_raw: Let ~ be an open equivalence relation on a topological space S with projection pi. If B={B_a} is a basis for S, then {pi(B_a)} is a basis for S/~.
nodes.text_normalized: Let ~ be an open equivalence relation on a topological space S with projection pi. If B={B_a} is a basis for S, then {pi(B_a)} is a basis for S/~.

nodes.label: Corollary 7.10
nodes.text_raw: If ~ is an open equivalence relation on a second-countable space S, then S/~ is second countable.
nodes.text_normalized: If ~ is an open equivalence relation on a second-countable space S, then S/~ is second countable.

nodes.label: Exercise 7.11
nodes.title: Real projective space as a quotient of a sphere
nodes.text_raw: For x=(x1,...,xn) in R^n ... real projective space as a quotient of a sphere.
nodes.text_normalized: For x=(x1,...,xn) in R^n ... real projective space as a quotient of a sphere.

nodes.text_raw: The real projective line RP^1 ...
nodes.text_normalized: The real projective line RP^1 ...

nodes.label: Example 7.13
nodes.title: The real projective plane RP2
nodes.text_raw: By Exercise 7.11, there is a homeomorphism ... real projective plane RP^2 (see Problem 7.2).
nodes.text_normalized: By Exercise 7.11, there is a homeomorphism ... real projective plane RP^2 (see Problem 7.2).

nodes.label: Proposition 7.14
nodes.text_raw: The equivalence relation ~ on R^{n+1}-{0} in the definition of RP^n is open.
nodes.text_normalized: The equivalence relation ~ on R^{n+1}-{0} in the definition of RP^n is open.

nodes.label: Corollary 7.15
nodes.text_raw: The real projective space RP^n is second countable.
nodes.text_normalized: The real projective space RP^n is second countable.

nodes.label: Proposition 7.16
nodes.text_raw: The real projective space RP^n is Hausdorff.
nodes.text_normalized: The real projective space RP^n is Hausdorff.
